mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real GDP expands corporate earnings potential, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in growth periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Rapid federal funds rate hikes compress equity valuations by increasing the discount rate, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate signal a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate profits, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative median forward returns over a 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates strong manufacturing output and capacity utilization, boosting equity returns over the next 20 trading days specifically during late-cycle expansions when supply constraints are easing.
failure_condition: Signal fails to predict positive returns when INDPRO growth exceeds 1.5% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below 1.3 million annualized units signals weakening construction demand and consumer confidence, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns remain positive in >55% of months where starts fall below threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer credit balances alongside accelerating inflation erode real disposable income and purchasing power, suppressing corporate revenue growth and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal shows no statistically significant negative correlation with forward returns (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month increases in the consumer price index signal persistent inflationary pressure, prompting market repricing of higher discount rates and yielding negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive CPI prints consistently coincide with positive SPX forward returns over a 1-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 5.0% indicates deteriorating labor market conditions that typically precede earnings contractions, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days specifically during early-cycle recessions.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are positive in >50% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 5.0% during recessionary periods.
